title: 1950s America
description: Check your understanding of American politics and society in the 1950s.

assessment_items.question_content:
When North Korean communist leader **Kim Il Sung** attempted to reunify Korea under a communist government by invading the South, how did the US government respond?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: After World War II, the United States abandoned isolationism as a foreign policy.

choices.content: The US government rallied the UN Security Council to declare support for South Korea, and deployed an American-led UN coalition.
The US government ordered the military to detonate a nuclear bomb on the North Korean capital of Pyongyang
The US government declared its intention to resume its policy of isolationism in world affairs

assessment_items.question_content:
Why was Korea divided at the **38th Parallel**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The citizens of Korea did not vote t divide the country at the 38th parallel.

choices.content: When Korea was liberated from Japanese control after the Second World War, it was divided in half; the Soviet Union supported the North, and the United States supported the South.
A mountain range following approximately that line made a natural boundary between the two nations.
Korean citizens voted in 1950 to divide the country into two separate nations.

assessment_items.question_content:
The National Security Council released a report in 1950 known as **NSC-68**, which described the tensions between the United States and the USSR as

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: NSC-68 did not suggest that the tensions between the United States and the USSR were overblown by the media.

choices.content: an apocalyptic struggle between good and evil
the result of fundamental misunderstandings that could be remedied through better diplomacy
overblown by the media

assessment_items.question_content:
The **House Un-American Activities Committee** was a congressional body that

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The House Un-American Activities Committee did not attempt to outlaw foreign language classes.

choices.content: investigated Hollywood creatives and blacklisted alleged communists
sought to promote more cultural understanding between the United States and the Soviet Union
attempted to outlaw “un-American” education such as foreign language classes

assessment_items.question_content:
**George Kennan**, the American ambassador to Moscow, is perhaps best known for his proposed policy of

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Vietnamization, or turning the war effort in Vietnam over to Vietnamese soldiers, did not become a US policy until the presidency of Richard Nixon in the late 1960s.

choices.content: Containment
Massive retaliation
Vietnamization

assessment_items.question_content:
Senator **Joseph McCarthy** rose to prominence by alleging

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: McCarthy did not allege that the US government had prior knowledge of the attack at Pearl Harbor.

choices.content: that he was in possession of a list of known Communists working in the State Department.
that the US government had prior knowledge of the attack at Pearl Harbor and allowed it to occur in order to bring the nation into World War II.
that President Truman’s campaign operatives had tampered with the results of the election of 1948.

hints: The Monroe Doctrine, not the Truman Doctrine, concerned the interference of European powers in the Americas.

choices.content: the United States would help free peoples everywhere resist communism
European powers should not interfere in North or South America
the allies of the US should henceforward take responsibility for their own defense

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following slogans characterized Eisenhower's **New Look** foreign policy?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The United States did not commit to intervening in all international conflicts.

choices.content: "More bang for the buck" - a strategy to save money by relying on a stockpile of nuclear weapons rather than a large standing army
"The buck stops here" - a policy that committed the United States to intervening in all international conflicts
"Pass the buck" - a strategy to save money by asking the United States' allies to handle all international conflicts

assessment_items.question_content:
The Soviets’ 1957 launch of the satellite **_Sputnik I_** marked the beginning of

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: the Space Race between the United States and the Soviet Union
Improved relations between the United States and the Soviet Union
the Atomic Age
